Global (All).title: Cybersecurity breach at a major tech firm
Global (All).summary: Hackers gain access to sensitive customer data, causing widespread concern

Global (All).title: Tensions rise between two neighboring countries
Global (All).summary: Military buildup and diplomatic tensions escalate the risk of conflict

Global (All).title: Pandemic-related travel restrictions reintroduced
Global (All).summary: New variants and surging case numbers lead to increased border controls

United States.title: Riot in Washington D.C. following Supreme Court Decision
United States.summary: Massive protests and clashes with police erupted in Washington D.C. after the Supreme Court's decision on reproductive rights.

United States.title: Cyberattack on Colonial Pipeline
United States.summary: The Colonial Pipeline, which transports fuel along the East Coast, was hit by a cyberattack, leading to fuel shortages and supply chain disruptions.

United States.title: Shooting in New York City
United States.summary: A shooting in Times Square, New York City, left several people injured, and raised concerns about public safety and gun violence.

South Korea.title: South Korea reports first suspected case of monkeypox
South Korea.summary: South Korea has confirmed its first suspected case of monkeypox. The Ministry of Health and Welfare said the patient, who is a foreigner, was hospitalised after showing symptoms of monkeypox. The ministry has issued a warning to the public to be cautious, but is yet to confirm whether it is a case of monkeypox.

South Korea.title: Moon Jae-in calls for calm in face of North Korean missile test
South Korea.summary: Former South Korean President Moon Jae-in has called for calm after North Korea conducted a series of missile tests on Wednesday. Moon urged South Korean citizens to avoid any unnecessary movements and follow the government's instructions. The South Korean military responded to the missile tests by putting its troops on high alert.

South Korea.title: South Korea and US launch joint maritime drills in Yellow Sea
South Korea.summary: South Korea and the United States have launched a series of joint maritime drills in the Yellow Sea. The drills, which involve warships and aircraft from both countries, aim to demonstrate the two nations' commitment to regional security. The drills have been criticized by North Korea, which views them as a threat to its national security.

Japan.title: Japan PM Fumio Kishida's support slips amid scandal
Japan.summary: Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida's approval ratings fall to a new low amid the ongoing scandals surrounding him.

Japan.title: Japanese Fugitive Arrested in US for Alleged $14.7 Million Investment Scam
Japan.summary: A Japanese fugitive has been arrested in the US for allegedly running a $14.7 million investment scam targeting Japanese citizens.

Japan.title: Tokyo train collision probe finds 'no clear cause' yet
Japan.summary: The investigation into a recent train collision in Tokyo continues, with no clear cause yet identified, but initial findings suggest possible human error.
